other_observers: Nick Komar; Found by Ted Floyd and several others saw the bird in the several days that it stuck around.
specific_location: On the southwest shoreline of Baseline Reservoir
description: When I saw the bird it was very white bird with a gray back swimming circles close to the shore. Then I saw a black cap with a white forehead. Also saw a thick eyeline. The way it was holding the short thick bill was very distinct also, with the head up high and the bill turned downward. The bill was mostly black with a little bit of yellow on the lower mandible. It also had greenish legs.Behaviors: It was mostly on the muddy shoreline and swimming right next to the shore.Call: Never heard itPlumage: Juvenile
